stale_prose_lint.note: UNRESOLVABLE R3 count-locked at lib/test/test_reception_identity.py:497 ('four untested arms'); corroborated by comment-analyzer as documented_falsehood

completeness_critic_3_1_5.population: every command head inside a ```bash fence within the '## Reception Preflight' section of skills/receiving-code-review/SKILL.md
completeness_critic_3_1_5.property: no head outside the permitted set (illegal count == 0)
completeness_critic_3_1_5.independent_signal: python markdown-structure parse of the section's fenced blocks (NOT the audit's awk/sed/grep pipeline), cross-checked against the skill's own prose-declared permitted set
completeness_critic_3_1_5.result: audit's permitted set is a superset of the independent enumeration (11 statement heads, all covered)
completeness_critic_3_1_5.caveat: the audit asserts PERMITTED, not INVOCABLE - it is satisfied by the bare-basename form that no runtime path can execute (see F-1). Permitting is not invocability; the completeness property as stated holds, but the guard does not catch an unrunnable prescribed command.

phase3_findings.description: skills/receiving-code-review/SKILL.md prescribes a BARE BASENAME 'reception-record.py record' with no portable ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} anchor. Not on PATH locally; does not match the granted cloud literal Bash(.devflow/vendor/devflow/scripts/reception-record.py:*). Feature is inert on every tier, silently and permanently (degraded arm is non-blocking), so facts 10/11 always render missing and no session artifact is ever written. pf545_illegal_count's optional path group permits the bare form, so the detector is satisfied by an unrunnable command; the #275 P3 anchor pin is presence-only.

reception_identity.py calls .decode('utf-8') outside any except (lines ~101, ~122). A non-UTF-8 git-dir path raises UnicodeDecodeError, which is NOT an IdentityError; cmd_record catches only ri.IdentityError, so it escapes main() as a raw traceback. This falsifies the module docstring's 'Every failure mode raises IdentityError with a named reason and yields no identity'.

reception-record.py docstring states the ignore precondition runs 'Before writing any artifact', but _check_ignored is called only in cmd_record; cmd_append_disposition writes findings_path with no ignore check. --session-dir is per-invocation, so an append pointed at a non-ignored dir writes a TRACKED artifact - the self-invalidating-identity condition the precondition exists to prevent.

test_reception_identity.py:14 module docstring claims coverage of 'every derivation failure mode -> named breadcrumb, no printed identity'. Only 2 of 6 raise sites are exercised; the diff's own deferral block names three of the untested arms. Absolute claim contradicted within the same diff.

test_reception_identity.py:18 claims 'the six-shape adversarial matrix over each JSON artifact read back'; the matrix covers only the append-disposition findings read-back, as line 499 of the same diff states.

test_reception_identity.py:497 deferral block says 'four untested arms' then enumerates six. Self-referential ordinal count - the CLAUDE.md non-demotable self-contradicting-diff class. INTRODUCED BY THIS RUN'S OWN FIX COMMIT 6e1de99a.

Token-rebinding behaviour has zero coverage. test_idempotent_record asserts only the ledger half; nothing asserts the identity changes after an edit nor stays equal for an unchanged tree. A regression reusing the recorded identity would pass the whole suite. Ties to checklist FAIL VC-34 (AC says 'idempotent for the identity artifact'; code re-derives and overwrites).

The CLI is never driven as a subprocess - every test calls rr.main(argv) in-process. Shebang, exec bit, SystemExit(main()) and the sys.path bootstrap are untested. Directly related to F-1: a subprocess-form test would likely have exposed the uninvocable prescribed command.

rcv/#668 P-nooutput is a behavioural pin carrying only assert_pin_unique, no assert_pin_red_under mutation evidence - unlike its sibling #545 P-stale-mp. Repo behavioural-fix-pin rule requires the mutation.

_check_ignored's undecidable arm is correctly fail-closed but its breadcrumb collapses three distinct operator situations (git absent, not-a-repo rc 128, internal error) into one non-actionable message with stderr routed to DEVNULL.

os.chmod(parent, DIR_MODE) failure swallowed with a bare pass and no breadcrumb on any channel. A group/world-writable directory permits unlink-and-replace of the identity artifact regardless of the 0600 file mode.
_read_json_object's 'empty' and 'unreadable:<class>' reasons are reachable but covered by neither the matrix nor the deferral record, which claims to enumerate the untested arms.
unknown_channel branch is unreachable through main() (argparse choices=CHANNELS rejects first); the deferral record mislabels it as an untested arm rather than dead defence-in-depth.
IdentityError.reason's documented closed vocabulary is expressed nowhere in code and enforced nowhere. Self-disclosed in the diff's own deferral block; sole consumer treats it as an opaque string.

lib/capability-profiles.json em-dashes re-serialized to \u2014, indicating a json.dump round-trip of a hand-maintained policy file. Diff noise on future edits. (Verified harmless: region_sha hashes only the token list, never the readme.)

verdict_grounds: Phase 4.2 rules 1/2: two checklist FAILs (VC-32, VC-34) after normalization
Self-contradicting-diff carve-out (non-demotable, every threshold): five documented_falsehood findings F-2..F-6
F-1 corroborated Important: the issue's entire deliverable is uninvocable on every tier
notes: The prior cloud review's Critical (flight-key misattribution) IS fixed - comment-analyzer independently re-verified the rewritten bullet true against HEAD.
F-6 was introduced by this run's own fix commit 6e1de99a - caught by Phase 0.6 lint and corroborated by comment-analyzer.
Step 0.5 deviation: worktree branch worktree-pr-681 tracks origin/claude/issue-668; HEAD == PR headRefOid so the invariant holds. gh pr checkout not run.
Phase 1.2 delivery deviation: issue context handed off by file reference rather than inlined (36KB), matching Phase 1.1's no-orchestrator-transit rule.
Dirty-tree backstop: snapshot OID e69de29b (clean tree) captured before Phase 3.1 dispatch.

stale_prose_check.note: post-fix HEAD clean; the iter-0 count-locked row is resolved

sweep_evidence.note: fix-delta scrutinised inline; caught two self-introduced defects before commit (a NameError from an out-of-scope cwd in the new append guard, and an incomplete IdentityError reason vocabulary after adding git_output_not_utf8)

fix_decisions.evidence: operator-chosen resolution: surface the rebind rather than change the code or amend the AC

fix_decisions.claim_text: SESSION_DIRNAME os.path.join backslash separators break check-ignore on Windows
fix_decisions.evidence: verifier established the opposite fail-direction: _check_ignored returns None on any unresolvable rc/OSError and cmd_record refuses before any write, so a Windows path problem fails CLOSED

parking_evidence.basis: the claim's premise is unsubstantiated; the code fails closed on the cited input
parking_evidence.failing_input: a backslash-separated session path passed to git check-ignore

fix_decisions.evidence: real but diagnostic-only; the arm is correctly fail-closed. Operator chose blockers+test-gaps scope. Revisit when an operator reports an unattributable ignore_check_failed.

fix_decisions.claim_text: chmod failure swallowed with no breadcrumb
fix_decisions.evidence: real; 0600 file mode still guards contents, directory-namespace substitution is the residual. Revisit if session artifacts become a trust root for a consumer.
both arms fail closed with named reasons. Revisit when the record read-back gains matrix coverage.
dead defence-in-depth behind argparse choices=. Revisit if the function gains a non-CLI caller.
self-disclosed; sole consumer treats reason as opaque. Revisit if reasons become a consumed API.
cosmetic diff noise; region_sha hashes only the token list, never the readme. Revisit on the next manifest edit.
